body.title: Igor watchdog: detect utility_closet_server down, relaunch
body.result: UtilityClosetWatchdog shipped — slow-tier push source, two-strike rule, 120s cooldown, IgorBase-inherited (avoids push_sources circular import). Live-validated: UC was dead at sprint start, relaunch_uc() brought it up (PID 1467112). 15 new tests pass.
body.description:
utility_closet_server (port 8080) currently dead; Igor's logs show 'Connection refused' every 5s on POST /api/agents/.../stats. Akien wants Igor to constantly nudge UC back to awake — relaunch when down.

Fix: new push source `UtilityClosetWatchdog` in wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources.py. Polls /api/health (or socket connect on 8080) every 60s. If down for 2 consecutive checks: relaunch via setsid (same pattern as ~/bin/igor launcher line 39). Cooldown: skip relaunch if last attempt < 120s ago to avoid restart storms. Logs to errors.log on failed relaunch.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources.py (new class + registration) + tests/test_uc_watchdog.py.
**Design rules:** inherit-base-class (BasePushSource); no-sqlite (n/a); approach-frame (positive: 'keep UC alive' framing, not 'don't let it die').
**Scope boundary:** in scope: monitoring + relaunch of utility_closet_server.py. Out of scope: monitoring other services, rewriting UC's startup flow.
**Test plan:** unit tests covering: down-detection, cooldown logic, no-restart-when-up, relaunch-command formatting. Live verify after deploy: kill UC manually, observe restart within 2-3 min.
